description: Sheet row count notifier: a form trigger kicks off a Google Sheets read, counts the returned rows, and using an IF node, routes to Slack when the count is greater than 2 or to Gmail otherwise.

conversation.text: Create a form trigger that takes in rows from a google sheet and if the count is greater than 2, then send message to slack, if not send email to gmail.

Plan: Form Trigger on submission → read rows from Google Sheets → count the rows → if count > 2, post a Slack message; otherwise send a Gmail email. Which spreadsheet and sheet should I read, which Slack channel should get the alert, and what Gmail address should receive the email?

text: For every HITL step during the build — keep the run moving without stalling. Allow all permissions. Use any of the existing credentials or skip.

conversation.text: I'll wire Form Trigger → Google Sheets read → count rows → if count > 2 send Slack, else send Gmail. Ready for me to build it?

outcomeExpectations: The workflow is triggered by a Form Trigger node (n8n-nodes-base.formTrigger) that starts when the form is submitted.
A Google Sheets node reads rows from a spreadsheet before the branching logic runs.
An if node branches on whether the row count is greater than 2.
When the count is greater than 2, the workflow sends a message via Slack.
When the count is 2 or less, the workflow sends an email via Gmail instead of Slack.

executionScenarios.name: row-count-above-threshold
executionScenarios.description: More than two sheet rows routes to Slack
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The Form Trigger receives a submission that kicks off the check. The Google Sheets read returns 3 rows (e.g. Name='Alice', Name='Bob', Name='Carol'). The row-count step outputs a count of 3. The decision node routes to the greater-than-2 branch. The Slack post-message node returns success.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors on the Slack path only: Form Trigger → Google Sheets read → decision branch (count > 2) → Slack message. A Slack message is sent about the row count being above the threshold. The Gmail node on the else branch is not executed.

executionScenarios.name: row-count-at-or-below-threshold
executionScenarios.description: Two or fewer sheet rows routes to Gmail
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The Form Trigger receives a submission that kicks off the check. The Google Sheets read returns 2 rows (e.g. Name='Alice', Name='Bob'). The row-count step outputs a count of 2. The decision node routes to the not-greater-than-2 branch. The Gmail send node returns success.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors on the Gmail path only: Form Trigger → Google Sheets read → decision branch (count ≤ 2) → Gmail send. An email is sent via Gmail about the row count being at or below the threshold. The Slack node on the greater-than-2 branch is not executed.
